title: Palicourea forsteronioides
intro: Palicourea forsteronioides, also known as Forster's palicourea, is a species of plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea forsteronioides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as palicourea alkaloids and quercetin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, as well as being used in traditional ceremonies and rituals.

This family consists of over 600 genera and more than 13,000 species. The genus Palicourea is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of the Americas, with a total of around 300 species.
